arts.ttl: Chelsea struggled without attacking trio - Maresca
arts.sum: Chelsea boss Enzo Maresca admits his side struggled for goals in their 1-0 loss to Arsenal without injured attacking players Cole Palmer, Nicolas Jackson and Noni Madueke.

arts.ttl: Chelsea: Cole Palmer to withdraw from Thomas Tuchel's first England squad
arts.sum: The Blues star missed the loss at Arsenal with a muscle injury

arts.ttl: Chelsea have become a chore to watch and Enzo Maresca had no answers against Arsenal
arts.sum: Arsenal beat Chelsea 1-0 thanks to a Mikel Merino goal, with Enzo Maresca's Blues turning in a toothless performance where they mustered just two shots on target

arts.ttl: Man City charges in full as timeline emerges with Arsenal, Chelsea and Tottenham watching on
arts.sum: Former City financial adviser Stefan Borson has recently claimed via Football Insider that the case will likely drag on until the end of this season if the club are found guilty. City manager Pep Guardiola revealed in February that he expects a decision 'in a month,' following the hearing that took place from September to December last year before an independent panel, during which the club vehemently denied all allegations.

arts.ttl: 'A really dangerous player': Mikel Arteta left seriously impressed by £31m Arsenal star in 1-0 win v Chelsea
Merino strike proves the difference as strikerless Arsenal sink Chelsea
arts.athr: Ed Aarons at the Emirates Stadium
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta had promised that Arsenal would not give up on their title dream easily and, although they remain 12 points behind the champions-elect Liverpool after one of the most comfortable 1-0 victories you will see, there remains hope in north London. Mikel Merino’s first-half goal - his first since 15 February in the league and Arsenal’s 11th of the season from a corner but first since New Year’s Day – proved enough to see off a toothless Chelsea side who looked badly short of inspiration without their talisman Cole Palmer to secure a first win in four league matches. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Arsenal ‘masters’ of set pieces as Chelsea boss accepts defeat
arts.sum: Chelsea manager Enzo Maresca declared Arsenal the “masters” of set pieces after they fell to a narrow defeat in the Premier League on Sunday. Makeshift forward Mikel Merino scored the only goal of a game in which the Blues failed to create any clear cut chances, flicking the ball beyond goalkeeper Robert Sanchez with his […] The post Arsenal ‘masters’ of set pieces as Chelsea boss accepts defeat first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: Arsenal player ratings vs Chelsea as Merino vindicates pick and another stars
arts.sum: Merino missed a chance for a second on the volley while Odegaard hit a tame effort into the arms of Sanchez but they eventually got the job done. In what was a delightfully feisty London derby between Arsenal and Chelsea, the Gunners took home the spoils on the afternoon.

arts.ttl: Arsenal 1-0 Chelsea: Enzo Maresca comments on ineffective attacking display, Cole Palmer injury blow
arts.sum: Chelsea head coach Enzo Maresca acknowledges that his side need to find an extra gear against teams competing at the top of the Premier League.

arts.ttl: Arteta praises Arsenal attitude in win over Chelsea
arts.sum: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta hails the attitude his side showed defensively in a hard-fought 1-0 win over Chelsea.
